sections.a_h1: Measure your project's viability before you build
sections.a_h2: 12-point framework that exposes weak spots in your idea — from validation to pricing to your own blind spots. Hard questions before you invest time and money.

answers.label: Not sure
answers.verdict: Check what you do not know. The project isn't ready yet.

answers.label: No
answers.verdict: KNOCKOUT — your project has a sickness. Full stop.

sections.incomplete: Rate all criteria to see your diagnosis.

items.a_h3: I can build and launch this in one week.
items.a_description: If it takes longer, the scope is too big. Simplify or postpone.

items.a_h3: Anyone gets the value without explanation.
items.a_description: If you need more than 30 seconds to explain it, rework.

items.a_h3: I can fund this myself, no investor needed.
items.a_description: If you need funding, you're playing a different game.

items.a_h3: I know exactly who this is for.
People need this and would pay for it today.
items.a_description: Ask real people — don't assume. Politeness is not validation.

items.a_h3: This will make money with a clear pricing model.
items.a_description: One-time sales are harder than recurring. Decide before launch.
Without an organic channel, you need a budget.

items.a_h3: This project is morally and ethically sound.
items.a_description: If you hesitate, the answer is no.

items.a_h3: I am not telling myself things I know aren't true.
items.a_description: If you can't disprove it with evidence, it's still a lie.

items.a_h3: My core assumptions are backed by evidence.
items.a_description: Until it's verified, it's just a story you're telling yourself.

items.a_h3: I can name three weaknesses honestly.
items.a_description: If each one starts with "but" — those are excuses.

items.a_h3: What remains without the lies and excuses?
items.a_description: If nothing remains, the idea isn't bad. It's just not ready yet.

seo.title: Sickness Labs — Diagnose What's Killing Your Indie Project
seo.description: Identify the diseases that kill side projects, startups, and indie products. From founder syndrome to feature creep — diagnose your project before it's too late.
